title: rubus hirtus var. cuneatus
intro: Rubus hirtus var. cuneatus, also known as the Japanese thimbleberry or thimbleberry, is a thorny shrub belonging to the rose family that is native to East Asia and Japan. The plant's medicinal properties include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Rubus hirtus var. cuneatus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
